title: notopleura parasitica subsp. parasitica
intro: Notopleura parasitica subsp. parasitica, also known as Notopleura, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Notopleura are attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of Plasmodium falciparum, the parasite responsible for malaria. Notopleura is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting overall well-being.
